description: Positioning food products as health and wellness alternatives by making comparative claims against supplements or conventional wellness products, emphasizing natural nutritional benefits.
structural_recipe: 1) Present a common wellness concern or product (e.g., collagen capsules). 2) Position your food product as a superior natural alternative. 3) Use casual, friendly tone with emojis. 4) Include implicit health benefit claims without clinical language. 5) Add lifestyle hashtags. Structure: Problem → Solution (your product) → Emotional benefit (enjoy/feel good).
why_it_works: Saudi audiences are health-conscious and skeptical of synthetic supplements. Positioning authentic local food products as natural wellness solutions leverages cultural preference for 'halal natural' eating while building premium positioning. Comparative framing creates urgency without hard-sell tactics.
transplantation_caveats: Health claims on food products are regulated in Saudi Arabia; avoid explicit medical/therapeutic claims that violate SFDA guidelines
Claims must not position food as drug alternative or treatment
Cultural sensitivity: wellness claims should emphasize 'natural' and 'traditional' rather than 'modern supplement replacement'
cultural_constraints: Avoid suggesting food replaces medication or prescribed supplements
Frame as lifestyle choice rather than medical solution
Emphasize heritage/traditional nutritional value when applicable
